elements.sent-2411-001: The problem of finding the sum of a polynomial's values is considered.
elements.sent-2411-002: In particular, for any $n\geq 3$, the explicit formula for the sum of the $n$th powers of natural numbers $S_n=\sum_{x=1 ^{m x^{n}$ is proved: \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}=(-1)^{n}m(m+1)(-\frac{1}{2}+\sum_{i=2}^{n}a_i(m+2)(m+3)...(m+i)),\end{equation} here $a_i=\frac{1 {i+1}\sum_{k=1 ^{i}\frac{(-1)^{k k^{n {k!(i-k)!}$, $(i=2,3,...,n-1)$, $a_n=\frac{(-1)^n {n+1}$.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Note that this formula does not contain Bernoulli numbers.
elements.sent-2411-004: For $n=1, 2, 3, 4$, the explicit formulas for the sum of powers of natural numbers \begin{equation}S_n=\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}\end{equation} were well-known already in the ancient times.
elements.sent-2411-005: In 1613, Faulhaber published a paper, where some properties of the function $S_n(m)$ were studied and, moreover, the explicit formulas for high odd values of $n$ were given \cite{F .}
elements.sent-2411-006: Nowadays, for calculating $S_n$, there are various recurrent formulas, geometric and matrix methods, as well as formulas that are valid for arbitrary $n$, but contain Bernoulli numbers (see, e.g., \cite{BV , \cite{E , \cite{Ge , \cite{Ka , \cite{MS , \cite{Ti , \cite{T , \cite{W , \cite{K ).}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-007: In \cite{GR , the explicit formulas for $S_n$ are given for the cases $n=1, 2,...,7$.}
elements.sent-2411-008: In the present work, we give an explicit formula for $S_n$, for an arbitrary natural $n$; this formula does not contain Bernoulli numbers.
elements.sent-2411-009: To this end, we first find the explicit formula for the function \begin{equation}g(m)=\sum_{x=1}^{m}f(x),\end{equation} where $f(x)$ is a polynomial.
elements.sent-2411-010: The found formula implies that $g(m)$ is a polynomial and is divisible by $m$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Applying again the found formula for $g(m)$, the explicit formula for the sum of the $n$th powers of natural numbers is obtained: \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}=(-1)^{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}a_im(m+1)(m+2)...(m+i),\end{equation} where \begin{equation}a_i=\frac{1}{i+1}\sum_{k=1}^{i}\frac{(-1)^{k}k^{n}}{k!(i-k)!},\end{equation} $(i=1, 2,...,n)$.
elements.sent-2411-012: Note also that, for the same sum, the following formula was given without proof in our earlier paper \cite{S : \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}=\sum_{i=1}^{n}\sum_{k=1}^{i}\frac{(-1)^{k+n}k^{n}m(m+1)(m+2)...(m+i)C_i^k}{(i+1)!},\end{equation} where $C_i^k$ denotes the binomial coefficient ${\begin{pmatrix i k \end{pmatrix }$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Taking the fact that the principal term of the polynomial \begin{equation}S_n(m)=\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}\end{equation} is $\frac{1 {n+1 m^{n+1}$ \cite{K , \cite{BSh , the calculation of $a_n$ can be simplified.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-015: The above-mentioned formulas imply that if $n\geq 3$, then \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}=(-1)^{n}m(m+1)(-\frac{1}{2}+\sum_{i=2}^{n}a_i(m+2)(m+3)...(m+i)),\end{equation} where $a_i$ is given by (1.2), for $i=2,3,...,n-1$, while $a_n$ is given by (1.3).
elements.section-2411-002: The sum of powers of natural numbers
elements.sent-2411-016: Below we give the formula for the sum of the values of a polynomial $f(x)$ in the points $x=1,2,...,m$.
elements.sent-2411-017: To this end, we first give the following lemma.
elements.sent-2411-018: Consider the polynomial \begin{equation}h(x)=l_0+\sum_{i=1}^{n}l_ix(x+1)(x+2)...(x+i-1),\end{equation} and choose coefficients $l_0$, $l_1$, ..., $l_n$ in such a way that the values of the polynomials $f(x)$ and $h(x)$ coincide in the $(n+1)$ points: \begin{equation}f(-j)=h(-j)\; \; \; \; (j=0,1,2,...,n).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-020: Applying the well-known formula \begin{equation}C_k^0-C_k^1+C_k^2+...+(-1)^kC_k^k=0,\end{equation} ($k\in \mathbb{N}$) where $C_i^j$ denotes the binomial coefficient ${\begin{pmatrix j i \end{pmatrix }$ , it is not hard to verify that the solution of the system (2.4) is \vskip+2mm \begin{equation}l_0=f(0), \; \; \; l_i=\sum_{k=0}^{i}\frac{(-1)^kf(-k)}{k!(i-k)!} \; \; \; (i=1,2,...,n).\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-022: Therefore, Lemma 2.1 implies \begin{theo \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}f(x)=mf(0)+\sum_{i=1}^{n}\frac{d_i}{i+1}m(m+1)(m+2)...(m+i),\end{equation} where $d_i$ ($i=1,2,...,n)$ is given by formula (2.2).}
elements.sent-2411-023: Note that it does not contain Bernoulli numbers.
elements.sent-2411-025: Taking formula (2.7) and the fact that the principal term of the polynomial $S_n(m)$ is $\frac{1 {n+1 m^{n+1}$ \cite{K , \cite{BSh , the calculation of $a_n$ can be simplified.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-026: From Theorem 2.4, formulas (2.9) and (2.10), we obtain \begin{theo Let $n\geq 3$.}
elements.sent-2411-027: Then one has \begin{equation}\sum_{x=1}^{m}x^{n}=(-1)^{n}m(m+1)(-\frac{1}{2}+\sum_{i=2}^{n}a_i(m+2)(m+3)...(m+i)),\end{equation} where $a_i$ is given by formula (2.8) for $i=2,3,...,n-1$, while $a_n$ is given by (2.10).
